substrate_description: 3-node binary deterministic substrate following the Rule 110 cellular-automaton logic, fully connected. Not a published worked example, so the only independent reference is a genuine PyPhi 1.x IIT 3.0 SIA.
context: Independent reference for the rule110_iit3_emd golden fixture (test/data/golden/v1/rule110_iit3_emd.json). The 2.0 golden's sia.phi depends on the load-bearing PURVIEW_SIZE purview tie-break (see tie_break_sensitivity below); this file pins it against a correctly-invoked PyPhi 1.2.0 IIT 3.0 SIA so the golden is anchored to an external implementation, not to PyPhi reproducing PyPhi.

control.note: The same PyPhi 1.2.0 oracle reproduces the independently-anchored basic value (2.3125 = 37/16, basic_sia_phi_canonical.json) exactly, with the matching cut [B,C]--/-->[A] and concept set {B, C, AB, ABC}. This establishes that the 1.x harness is correctly invoked before it is trusted on rule110.

config.note: Matches the 2.0 golden config: EMD mechanism + EMD CES-distance, JOINT_BIPARTITION mechanism partitions, DIRECTED_BIPARTITION system cuts, precision 6. PICK_SMALLEST_PURVIEW=false (PyPhi 1.x's 10-year default) corresponds to 2.0's purview_tie_resolution=[PHI, PURVIEW_SIZE] resolved with operation=max (prefer the larger purview).

tie_break_sensitivity.summary: The purview tie-break is load-bearing for the system phi, confirming the roadmap audit. Resolving purview ties toward the larger purview (the 2.0 PURVIEW_SIZE / 1.x default) gives sia.phi = 1.100015; resolving toward the smaller purview gives 1.033765.
